text: Anatomy
the study of the structure of living bodies
original_text: Anatomy is the study of the structure of living bodies.

text: human anatomy studies the parts of the body and how they are organized
original_text: Human anatomy studies the parts of the body and how they are organized.

text: focuses on structure, while physiology focuses on function
original_text: Anatomy focuses on structure, while physiology focuses on function.

text: the body is organized from cells to tissues to organs to organ systems
original_text: The body is organized from cells to tissues to organs to organ systems.

text: cells are the smallest living units of the body
original_text: Cells are the smallest living units of the body.

text: tissues are groups of similar cells working together
original_text: Tissues are groups of similar cells working together.

text: organs are structures made of multiple tissue types
original_text: Organs are structures made of multiple tissue types.

text: organ systems are groups of organs that perform major body functions
original_text: Organ systems are groups of organs that perform major body functions.

text: the skeletal system supports the body and protects internal organs
original_text: The skeletal system supports the body and protects internal organs.

text: bones provide structure, protection, mineral storage, and blood cell production
original_text: Bones provide structure, protection, mineral storage, and blood cell production.

text: joints are places where bones meet
original_text: Joints are places where bones meet.

text: ligaments connect bones to other bones
original_text: Ligaments connect bones to other bones.

text: cartilage cushions joints and provides flexible support
original_text: Cartilage cushions joints and provides flexible support.

text: the skull protects the brain
original_text: The skull protects the brain.

text: the rib cage protects the heart and lungs
original_text: The rib cage protects the heart and lungs.

text: the spine supports the body and protects the spinal cord
original_text: The spine supports the body and protects the spinal cord.

text: the muscular system allows movement, posture, and heat production
original_text: The muscular system allows movement, posture, and heat production.

text: skeletal muscles attach to bones and help move the body
original_text: Skeletal muscles attach to bones and help move the body.

text: tendons connect muscles to bones
original_text: Tendons connect muscles to bones.

text: smooth muscle is found in organs such as the stomach and intestines
original_text: Smooth muscle is found in organs such as the stomach and intestines.

text: cardiac muscle is found in the heart
original_text: Cardiac muscle is found in the heart.

text: the nervous system controls communication throughout the body
original_text: The nervous system controls communication throughout the body.

text: the brain is the body's main control center
original_text: The brain is the body's main control center.

text: the spinal cord carries signals between the brain and body
original_text: The spinal cord carries signals between the brain and body.

text: nerves carry information to and from the central nervous system
original_text: Nerves carry information to and from the central nervous system.

text: the circulatory system transports blood, oxygen, nutrients, hormones, and wastes
original_text: The circulatory system transports blood, oxygen, nutrients, hormones, and wastes.

text: the heart pumps blood through blood vessels
original_text: The heart pumps blood through blood vessels.

text: arteries usually carry blood away from the heart
original_text: Arteries usually carry blood away from the heart.

text: veins usually carry blood back toward the heart
original_text: Veins usually carry blood back toward the heart.

text: capillaries allow exchange between blood and tissues
original_text: Capillaries allow exchange between blood and tissues.

text: the respiratory system brings oxygen into the body and removes carbon dioxide
original_text: The respiratory system brings oxygen into the body and removes carbon dioxide.

text: the lungs are the main organs of breathing
original_text: The lungs are the main organs of breathing.

text: the diaphragm helps move air into and out of the lungs
original_text: The diaphragm helps move air into and out of the lungs.

text: alveoli are tiny air sacs where gas exchange occurs
original_text: Alveoli are tiny air sacs where gas exchange occurs.

text: the trachea carries air toward the lungs
original_text: The trachea carries air toward the lungs.

text: the digestive system breaks food into nutrients the body can use
original_text: The digestive system breaks food into nutrients the body can use.

text: the mouth begins mechanical and chemical digestion
original_text: The mouth begins mechanical and chemical digestion.

text: the esophagus moves food from the mouth to the stomach
original_text: The esophagus moves food from the mouth to the stomach.

text: the stomach mixes food with acid and enzymes
original_text: The stomach mixes food with acid and enzymes.

text: the small intestine absorbs most nutrients
original_text: The small intestine absorbs most nutrients.

text: the large intestine absorbs water and forms waste
original_text: The large intestine absorbs water and forms waste.

text: the liver processes nutrients and produces bile
original_text: The liver processes nutrients and produces bile.

text: the pancreas produces digestive enzymes and hormones
original_text: The pancreas produces digestive enzymes and hormones.

text: the gallbladder stores bile
original_text: The gallbladder stores bile.

text: the urinary system removes wastes from the blood and balances fluids
original_text: The urinary system removes wastes from the blood and balances fluids.

text: the kidneys filter blood and produce urine
original_text: The kidneys filter blood and produce urine.

text: the bladder stores urine before it leaves the body
original_text: The bladder stores urine before it leaves the body.

text: the endocrine system uses hormones to regulate body processes
original_text: The endocrine system uses hormones to regulate body processes.

text: hormones are chemical messengers released by glands
original_text: Hormones are chemical messengers released by glands.

text: the pituitary gland helps regulate other endocrine glands
original_text: The pituitary gland helps regulate other endocrine glands.

text: the thyroid gland helps regulate metabolism
original_text: The thyroid gland helps regulate metabolism.

text: the adrenal glands release hormones involved in stress responses
original_text: The adrenal glands release hormones involved in stress responses.

text: the immune system protects the body from disease
original_text: The immune system protects the body from disease.

text: white blood cells help identify and fight threats
original_text: White blood cells help identify and fight threats.

text: the lymphatic system helps move fluid and supports immune defense
original_text: The lymphatic system helps move fluid and supports immune defense.

text: the skin is the body's largest organ
original_text: The skin is the body's largest organ.

text: skin protects the body, senses the environment, and helps regulate temperature
original_text: Skin protects the body, senses the environment, and helps regulate temperature.

text: hair and nails are structures associated with the integumentary system
original_text: Hair and nails are structures associated with the integumentary system.

text: the reproductive system produces sex cells and supports reproduction
original_text: The reproductive system produces sex cells and supports reproduction.

text: the body has many feedback systems that help maintain stability
original_text: The body has many feedback systems that help maintain stability.

text: the eyes detect light and allow vision
original_text: The eyes detect light and allow vision.

text: the ears detect sound and help with balance
original_text: The ears detect sound and help with balance.

text: the nose helps detect smells and filters incoming air
original_text: The nose helps detect smells and filters incoming air.

text: the tongue helps with taste, speech, and moving food
original_text: The tongue helps with taste, speech, and moving food.

text: sensory receptors detect information from inside and outside the body
original_text: Sensory receptors detect information from inside and outside the body.

text: the abdomen contains organs such as the stomach, intestines, liver, and kidneys
original_text: The abdomen contains organs such as the stomach, intestines, liver, and kidneys.

text: the chest cavity contains the heart and lungs
original_text: The chest cavity contains the heart and lungs.

text: the pelvis supports lower body organs and connects the spine to the legs
original_text: The pelvis supports lower body organs and connects the spine to the legs.

text: the hands contain many bones, muscles, tendons, and nerves for precise movement
original_text: The hands contain many bones, muscles, tendons, and nerves for precise movement.

text: the feet support balance, movement, and body weight
original_text: The feet support balance, movement, and body weight.

text: anatomical position is the standard reference posture used to describe the body
original_text: Anatomical position is the standard reference posture used to describe the body.

text: superior means closer to the head
original_text: Superior means closer to the head.

text: inferior means farther from the head
original_text: Inferior means farther from the head.

text: anterior means toward the front of the body
original_text: Anterior means toward the front of the body.

text: posterior means toward the back of the body
original_text: Posterior means toward the back of the body.

text: medial means closer to the body's midline
original_text: Medial means closer to the body's midline.

text: lateral means farther from the body's midline
original_text: Lateral means farther from the body's midline.

text: proximal means closer to the point of attachment
original_text: Proximal means closer to the point of attachment.

text: distal means farther from the point of attachment
original_text: Distal means farther from the point of attachment.

text: uses precise terms so body locations can be described clearly
original_text: Anatomy uses precise terms so body locations can be described clearly.

text: organs work together rather than acting completely alone
original_text: Organs work together rather than acting completely alone.

text: structure and function are deeply connected in anatomy
original_text: Structure and function are deeply connected in anatomy.

text: the shape of a body part often reflects what it does
original_text: The shape of a body part often reflects what it does.

text: helps explain how injury or disease affects the body
original_text: Anatomy helps explain how injury or disease affects the body.

text: doctors, nurses, therapists, and scientists all rely on anatomy
original_text: Doctors, nurses, therapists, and scientists all rely on anatomy.

text: medical imaging helps reveal internal anatomy without surgery
original_text: Medical imaging helps reveal internal anatomy without surgery.

text: X-rays are useful for viewing bones and some internal structures
original_text: X-rays are useful for viewing bones and some internal structures.

text: MRI scans can show soft tissues in detail
original_text: MRI scans can show soft tissues in detail.

text: CT scans create detailed cross-sectional images of the body
original_text: CT scans create detailed cross-sectional images of the body.

text: ultrasound uses sound waves to create images of internal structures
original_text: Ultrasound uses sound waves to create images of internal structures.

text: makes the body feel less like a mystery box
original_text: Anatomy makes the body feel less like a mystery box.

text: every movement depends on many structures working together
original_text: Every movement depends on many structures working together.

text: the body is both sturdy and strangely delicate
original_text: The body is both sturdy and strangely delicate.

text: shows how organized the chaos of the body really is
original_text: Anatomy shows how organized the chaos of the body really is.

text: the same body system can affect many different parts of life
original_text: The same body system can affect many different parts of life.

text: anatomy the map of the human machine
original_text: The meatball considers anatomy the map of the human machine.

text: body architecture, except the building walks around and complains
original_text: Anatomy is body architecture, except the building walks around and complains.

text: turns bones, muscles, and organs into a readable system
original_text: Anatomy turns bones, muscles, and organs into a readable system.

text: anatomy proves that even ordinary movement is secretly complicated
original_text: Anatomy proves that even ordinary movement is secretly complicated.

text: is made of and how its parts fit together
original_text: At its core, anatomy asks what the body is made of and how its parts fit together.
